title: Executive Order 9808—Establishing the President's Committee on Civil Rights

flags.justification: A fifteen-member committee with broad, varied representation, purely advisory in function ('authorized... to inquire into and to determine') with no operational or enforcement authority.
The Committee's function is clearly bounded to inquiry and reporting; Sec. 6 provides for automatic dissolution upon report submission.
Single coherent purpose: civil rights inquiry and recommendations.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

Sec. 6 explicitly provides for automatic termination: 'upon rendition of its report to the President, the Committee shall cease to exist, unless otherwise determined by further Executive order' — a clean, self-limiting design.
Creates a new investigatory body; removes no existing oversight.
Sec. 4's compel-testimony power over federal employees gives the Committee real, if internal-to-government, investigatory capacity to gather information independent of voluntary agency disclosure.
The Committee's eventual report proved highly influential, but the order's own text is narrowly bounded — investigate, report, then dissolve — and claims no ongoing or reusable authority.
Sec. 3's cooperation directive to all executive departments is framed as information-sharing support, not mandate redirection.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. A historically pivotal order — its report ('To Secure These Rights') directly influenced Truman's own EO 9981 the following year — yet a clean, well-designed structure: self-terminating, purely advisory, broadly representative.
